name: Computational Electrochemistry (DFT)
description: Computational electrocatalysis models an electrode reaction with DFT and the computational hydrogen electrode: intermediate energies build a potential-dependent free-energy diagram, identifying the potential-determining step and theoretical overpotential to screen catalysts.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Electrode reaction of interest"]
    N2["Build slab + adsorbate model"]
    N3["Computational H electrode"]
    N4["DFT energies of intermediates"]
    N5["Apply −eU potential shift"]
    N6["Free-energy diagram vs U"]
    N7{"Potential-determining step?"}
    N8["Identify limiting step"]
    N9["Theoretical overpotential"]
    N10["Screen new catalyst"]
    N11["Activity prediction"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 -->|new model| N2
    N9 --> N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Origin of the overpotential for oxygen reduction at a fuel-cell cathode (CHE model)
Towards the computational design of solid catalysts

sources.title: Toward an atomic-scale understanding of electrochemical interface structure and dynamics
sources.journal: Journal of the American Chemical Society
